q: Anodising is generally done on which metal?
In the process of binary fission in unicellular organisms such as bacteria and protozoa, which of the following is least likely to occur?
o: Rapid population increase under favourable conditions
No need for a second parent

q: Which part of a flower has to be removed for cross-pollination?
Which of the following describes the nature of the average speed?
o: It is a vector quantity (magnitude and direction).
It is a scalar quantity (only magnitude).
It is always equal to or less than the average velocity.

q: A train is travelling at a speed of 180 km/hr. Brakes are applied so as to produce a uniform acceleration of (-1 m/s^2). What will be the distance covered by the train before coming to rest?
Which of the following changes, instead of a chemical change, is responsible for giving cooked food its golden-brown colour?
Which of the following statements is correct?
o: Work can be done without displacement.
Work requires both force and displacement.
Work can be done without force.

q: The heat produced in a conductor due to the flow of current does NOT depend on ______.
The filament of an electric bulb is made of tungsten because it:
o: has a high resistance and can withstand high temperatures

q: Which activity shows that particles of matter are continuously moving?
o: Sugar settling at the bottom of a glass of water
Water being heated to its boiling point
Fragrance of perfumes spreading in a room

q: Which of the following gases makes lime water turn milky?
o: Chlorine, which turns the lime water green by reacting with calcium
Hydrogen, which raises the pH of the lime water
Carbon dioxide, which forms calcium carbonate

q: Which statement accurately describes the characteristics of protons in an atom?
o: They revolve around the nucleus in fixed orbits.
They have negligible mass compared to electrons.
They are located in the nucleus of an atom.

q: Which part of the brain controls involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation, and vomiting?
Which of the following reactions correctly represents the synthesis of bleaching powder?
The hydrophilic end of a soap molecule is:
o: non-ionic and insoluble in oil and water

q: Predict the elements corresponding to the following numbers of protons and neutrons. A: 20 protons and 20 neutrons; B: 19 protons and 20 neutrons.
Which of the following statements about the physical properties of non-metals is correct?
o: Non-metals have high density and high melting points.
Non-metals are good conductors of electricity.
Non-metals are generally brittle in solid state.

q: In water purification, which of the following methods is used to remove dirt and tiny suspended particles from water?
o: Filtration through a fine cloth (Disposable plastic cups)

q: An object placed between P and 2P of a convex lens forms an image ______.

Which of the following statements is/are true about villi in the small intestine? Statements: A: Increase surface area for absorption; B: Contain blood vessels; C: Help in excretion; D: Absorb digested nutrients.
o: Only Statements A, B and D
Only Statements A, B and C

q: Which of the following is a heterogeneous mixture?
Asexual reproduction has differences from sexual reproduction. Which of the following is NOT representing asexual reproduction?
o: Involvement of both male and female reproductive parts

q: If a person weighing 600 N stands on two feet, the contact area of which is 0.05 m^2, then what will be the pressure exerted on the sand?
Which of the following simple permanent tissues have its cell walls lignified?
Which gland in the human body secretes the growth hormone?
A man bought goods worth Rs 14,000 in total and sold goods worth Rs 6,000 at a loss of 20%. He sold the remaining goods worth Rs 10,000 in total at a profit of 20%. Find his overall profit or loss.
Rs 3,787 is divided among A, B, C and D such that A : B, B : C and C : D are in the ratios 4 : 5, 4 : 5 and 6 : 7 respectively. Find the share of A.
A garrison of 1,206 soldiers had food supplies for 24 days. If the number of soldiers is increased by 1,809, then for how many days will the food last?
A certain sum of money becomes three times itself in 8 years at simple interest. In how many years will it become four times itself?
The volume of a solid cylinder is 34,650 cm^3. If its height is 25 cm, find the circumference of the base. (Use pi = 22/7)
A, B and C can complete a piece of work in 2, 8 and 9 days respectively. Working together for one day and then leaving thrice as fast, in how much time (in days) will they complete the work?
A car going at 36 km/hr meets another car coming from the opposite direction at 24 km/hr. Find the average speed (in km/hr) of the two cars during the entire journey.

A spherical ball of radius 36 cm is dropped into a cylindrical vessel of radius 6 cm partly filled with water. If the ball is completely submerged, then by how much (in cm) will the water level in the cylindrical vessel rise?
Three numbers are in the ratio 7 : 20 : 8 and the sum of these numbers is 4830. Find the middle number.
A cricketer has an average of 55 runs over 5 innings. If he scores 166 runs in the 6th inning, by how many runs (rounded off to the nearest whole number) does his average score increase?
A trader bought 20 kg of apples for Rs 3,600 and sold them at the rate of Rs 220/kg. Find his profit (in Rs).
If 50% of 80% of 1/2 of a number is 442, find the number.

Two friends start from the same point and travel in the same direction. One walks at a speed of 55 km/hr and the other walks at 72 km/hr. After 3 hours, what will be the distance (in km) between them?

A shopkeeper declares the following three schemes of discounts. Which scheme is the most beneficial to a customer? A. Buy 5 items and get 22 of the same items free; B. Two successive discounts of 13% and 38%; C. Two successive equal discounts of 21%.

Six years ago, the ratio of a mother's age to her daughter's age was 5 : 1, and after 2 years from now, the ratio of the mother's age to her daughter's age will be 3 : 1. Find the ratio of the mother's present age to her daughter's present age.
Simplify (24 - 6 x 3) + 18 + 3 x 2.
